channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Why 1000 != 1000 in Payments
item.description: Money is deceptively simple. You have an amount, you have a currency. How hard can it be? Turns out — very. A tiny mistake in how you represent a number can silently drain a business, trigger...

item.title: HTML is Not Just You Think...
item.description: 1. Introduction What is HTML? HTML (HyperText Markup Language) is the standard markup language used to create and structure web pages. It helps browsers display content such as text, images,...

item.title: Creativity Is Theft. And Nature Stole First.
item.description: Let me ask you something weird. Why does the human brain look like a neural network? Not metaphorically. Actually. Literally. The thing we built and called "artificial intelligence" — the thing...

item.title: Node Voltage Analysis: The Method That Makes Circuits Easy
item.description: Node Voltage Analysis: The Method That Makes Circuits Easy The first real analysis technique — and why it's just organized common sense If you've been following this series, you know voltage,...

item.title: Code Story: Optimizing Our PostgreSQL 16 Queries with EXPLAIN and Drizzle 0.30 Cut Response Time by 40%
item.description: At 2:14 PM on a Tuesday in Q3 2024, our production PostgreSQL 16 cluster hit a p99 query latency of 2.8 seconds for our core order lookup endpoint, triggering 12 customer support tickets in 10...

item.title: Shipping Paid MCP Tools on Base Mainnet: the Build Pattern and What I Got Wrong
item.description: A couple of months ago I shipped ForgePoint Signal — a regulatory monitoring MCP server with x402 micropayments on Base mainnet. The first post was about what I built and how. This one is the...

item.title: I Was a Human AI Agent Before it was Cool
item.description: In 2026, what I'm about to describe would be a three-tool agent loop. An LLM instance with computer use, a couple of MCP servers, maybe a router for the edge cases. A weekend project. When I...

item.title: Why Most ATS Systems Reject Good Resumes
item.description: Many job seekers believe their resume is strong enough for recruiters, but in reality, most applications are filtered by ATS (Applicant Tracking Systems) before a human even sees them. That means...

item.title: Aurora’s Chris Urmson on why self-driving trucks are finally ready to scale
item.description: Self-driving has been “almost here” for over a decade. But somewhere between DARPA challenges and a handful of driverless trucks hauling freight between Dallas and Houston, Aurora co-founder and...

item.title: OpenClaw and Claude can put your AI-generated podcasts in Spotify
item.description: Save to Spotify is a new command-line tool designed specifically for AI agents like OpenClaw, Claude Code, or OpenAI Codex. If you're the kind of person who collects research on a topic, then...

item.title: 2 days left: Get 50% off a second pass to TechCrunch Disrupt 2026
item.description: Two days left to save up to $410 on your pass, and get a second one at 50% off to TechCrunch Disrupt 2026. Offer ends May 8, 11:59 p.m. PT. Register now.

item.title: Google’s taking a big swing at AI health with the Fitbit Air
item.description: It's a Whoop dupe. That was my first thought when I saw the new $99 Google Fitbit Air. You can hardly blame me. The band is screenless with a metallic fabric clasp. My eyes flickered between the...

item.title: Ploopy’s new mouse makes the ThinkPad’s iconic TrackPoint portable
item.description: Ploopy has announced another open source mouse alternative that skips touchpads and trackballs. While the new Ploopy Bean looks like a tiny travel-friendly mouse, you don't need to move it around....

item.title: One Click, Total Shutdown: The "Patient Zero" Webinar on Killing Stealth Breaches
item.description: The hardest part of cybersecurity isn't the technology, it’s the people. Every major breach you’ve read about lately usually starts the same way: one employee, one clever email, and one "Patient...

item.title: China’s Moonshot AI raises $2B at $20B valuation as demand for open-source AI skyrockets
item.description: Moonshot's annualized recurring revenue topped $200 million in April, driven by rapid growth in paid subscriptions and API usage.

item.title: PAN-OS RCE Exploit Under Active Use Enabling Root Access and Espionage
item.description: Palo Alto Networks has disclosed that threat actors may have attempted to unsuccessfully exploit a recently disclosed critical security flaw as early as April 9, 2026. The vulnerability in...

item.title: Inside Dreame’s wild launch event — packed with products no one can buy
item.description: Hundreds of influencers, media folks, and a few C-list celebs (sorry, Woz) filled the cavernous halls of the San Francisco Palace of Fine Arts last week. They were assembled for Dreame's launch...

item.title: Spotify wants to become the home for AI-generated personal audio
item.description: Users will be able to create a podcast from Codex or Claude Code and import it to Spotify

item.title: Spotify’s AI DJ now supports French, German, Italian and Brazilian Portuguese
item.description: Spotify's AI DJ feature now supports French, German, Italian, and Brazilian Portuguese.

item.title: The Download: the tech reshaping IVF and the rise of balcony solar
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. What’s next for IVF IVF has brought millions of babies...

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: Edge Plaintext Passwords, ICS 0-Days, Patch-or-Die Alerts and 25+ New Stories
item.description: Bad week. Turns out the easiest way to get hacked in 2026 is still the same old garbage: shady packages, fake apps, forgotten DNS junk, scam ads, and stolen logins getting dumped into Discord...

item.title: Smart Glasses for the Authorities
item.description: ICE is developing its own version of smart glasses, with facial recognition tied to various databases.

item.title: Thousands of Vibe-Coded Apps Expose Corporate and Personal Data on the Open Web
item.description: Companies like Lovable, Base44, Replit, and Netlify use AI to let anyone build a web app in seconds—and in thousands of cases, spill highly sensitive data onto the public internet.

item.title: This Reggae Band Is in a Nightmare Battle Against AI Slop Remixes
item.description: When Stick Figure’s seven-year-old song shot up the charts, the band was thrilled. But its viral moment was spurred by unauthorized AI remixes.

item.title: Parloa builds service agents customers want to talk to
item.description: Parloa leverages OpenAI models to power scalable, voice-driven AI customer service agents, enabling enterprises to design, simulate, and deploy reliable, real-time interactions.

item.title: Day Zero Readiness: The Operational Gaps That Break Incident Response
item.description: Having an incident response retainer, or even a pre-approved external incident response firm, is not the same as being ready for an incident. A retainer means someone will answer the phone....

item.title: The balcony solar boom is coming to the US
item.description: Dozens of US states are considering legislation to allow people to install plug-in solar systems, often called balcony solar. These small arrays require little to no setup and could help cut...

item.title: PyPI Packages Deliver ZiChatBot Malware via Zulip APIs on Windows and Linux
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered three packages on the Python Package Index (PyPI) repository that are designed to stealthily deliver a previously unknown malware family called ZiChatBot...
